How to setup a github token in solveit

🤖Reply🤖
To set up a GitHub token in solveit, you’ll need to store it as an environment variable. Here’s how:
You can set it in your code using:
import os
os.environ['GITHUB_TOKEN'] = 'your_token_here'
However, for security reasons, you probably don’t want to hardcode your token directly in the notebook.
Do you already have a GitHub personal access token created, or do you need help creating one first?
How to change github remote url from http to ssh


🤖Reply🤖
To change your GitHub remote URL from HTTPS to SSH, you’ll use the git remote set-url command.
First, you can check your current remote URL:
!git remote -v
Then, to change it to SSH, you’ll need to know your repository’s SSH URL (which looks like git@github.com:username/repo.git).
Would you like me to show you the command to change it, or do you already know your SSH URL and want to try it yourself first?
Show me entire command


🤖Reply🤖
Here’s the command:
!git remote set-url origin git@github.com:username/repo.git
Replace username with your GitHub username and repo with your repository name.
If your remote is named something other than origin, replace that too.

title: solveit_z2h
text:
Developer Guide
If you are new to using nbdev here are some useful pointers to get you started.

Install solveit_z2h in Development mode
# make sure solveit_z2h package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to solveit_z2h
$ nbdev_prepare

title: solveit_z2h
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/Rahuketu86/solveit_z2h.git
or from conda
$ conda install -c Rahuketu86 solveit_z2h
or from pypi
$ pip install solveit_z2h


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages. Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on conda and pypi respectively.
